description: End-to-end answer-quality eval. Unlike gold_set.json (retrieval recall@k by page), this runs the full ask() pipeline (routing -> aggregation/semantic -> generation) and checks the ANSWER against ground truth verified from the parts/documents data. Assertions: must_include (all substrings present, case-insensitive), must_include_any (>=1 per group), expect_pages (subset of cited pages; only on lookups, where page-truth is clean), expected_mode ('lookup'|'aggregate'), must_refuse (out-of-domain).

items.question: What is the part number for the radio shelf?
What is the part number for the headliner hanger?
Where is the dorsal assembly listed?
What is the part number for the control yoke assembly?
What is the part number for the rear baggage compartment panel?
items.note: 0511470-4 is listed across the baggage-compartment pages 32-42

items.question: What is the part number for the true airspeed ring assembly?
What is the part number for the left main landing gear spring?
items.note: LH spring has sibling part numbers (0541198-5 / -9); accept the family

items.question: What is the part number for the overhead console support?
How many ribs per main wing side?
items.note: COUNT(DISTINCT station_num) of main ribs on the STANDARD wing config (figure 'Wing Structure Assembly', pp101/103) = 11 stations per side; matches RealitySearch's 10-11. The old '14' over-counted by mixing the long-range config and un-normalized station strings ('100'/'100.00'/'100.50').

items.question: List all adhesives used.
List all sealants used.
What is the most common fastener?
items.note: screws lead by catalog units (3145); honest answer notes rivets are under-counted

items.question: What is the most common fastener? Are Phillips screws used more than hex or torx?
items.note: drive types not in catalog: phillips=0 hex=1 torx=0

items.question: Which fastener is used more by quantity, screws or bolts?
items.note: generalization: screws 3145 units >> bolts 724; not one of the original 3

items.question: How many washers are used in total?
How many distinct screw part numbers are listed?
items.note: generalization: variety measure COUNT(DISTINCT part_number) WHERE '%SCREW%'

items.question: What assembly is the headliner hanger part of?
items.note: section context from the vision card

items.question: What is part C1-C2 used for?
items.note: Conley-Weld epoxy base adhesive for cementing plastic/fiberglass/metal

items.question: What is the capital of France?
How do I bake a chocolate cake?
How many bolts are used in the wing structure?
items.note: Scope-ambiguous (a client-clarification item): the literal 'Wing Structure Assembly' figure = 6 bolts (standard has 0, long-range 6), while a broad figure ILIKE '%wing%' = 38 but pulls in non-structural wing systems (strut, wing-leveler/autopilot). Both readings are defensible; accept either.

items.question: What is the part number of the left-hand wing rib assembly?
Are there more distinct screw part numbers or distinct bolt part numbers?
Are the headliner hanger and the radio shelf in the same assembly?
items.note: both in Fuselage Aft Section Assembly (pp 201-202)

items.question: What is the torque specification for the wing attach bolts?
items.note: a parts catalog has no torque specs; must not surface torque-tube parts as an answer

items.question: What is the part number for the GPS antenna?
items.note: no GPS in a 1963-74 catalog; must not invent a part number
